# terrible_community_general_ipa_sudorule (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cn` (String) Canonical name. Can not be changed as it is the unique identifier.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cmd` (String) List of commands assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all commands are removed from the rule. If option is omitted commands are not checked nor changed.
- `cmdcategory` (String) Command category the rule applies to.
- `cmdgroup` (String) List of command groups assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all command groups are removed from the rule. If option is omitted command groups are not checked nor changed.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `deny_cmd` (String) List of denied commands assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all commands are removed from the rule. If option is omitted commands are not checked nor changed.
- `deny_cmdgroup` (String) List of denied command groups assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all command groups are removed from the rule. If option is omitted command groups are not checked nor changed.
- `description` (String) Description of the sudo rule.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name竊致alue).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `host` (String) List of hosts assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all hosts are removed from the rule. If option is omitted hosts are not checked nor changed. Option `hostcategory` must be omitted to assign hosts.
- `hostcategory` (String) Host category the rule applies to. If `all` is passed one must omit `host` and `hostgroup`. Option `host` and `hostgroup` must be omitted to assign `all`.
- `hostgroup` (String) List of host groups assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all host groups are removed from the rule. If option is omitted host groups are not checked nor changed. Option `hostcategory` must be omitted to assign host groups.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `runasextusers` (String) List of external RunAs users.
- `runasgroupcategory` (String) RunAs Group category the rule applies to.
- `runasusercategory` (String) RunAs User category the rule applies to.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State to ensure.
- `sudoopt` (String) List of options to add to the sudo rule.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `user` (String) List of users assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all users are removed from the rule. If option is omitted users are not checked nor changed.
- `usercategory` (String) User category the rule applies to.
- `usergroup` (String) List of user groups assigned to the rule. If an empty list is passed all user groups are removed from the rule. If option is omitted user groups are not checked nor changed.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `sudorule` (String) Sudorule as returned by IPA.
